answer:
\boxed{B}

The data shows **moderate agreement** (kappa coefficients of 0.52 for collisions and 0.47 for convictions) but also significant discrepancies—13.5% for collisions and 17.3% for convictions did not match between sources. When both records were combined, up to **56% more accidents and 46% more convictions** were identified, indicating substantial inconsistency. Thus, they **do not fully agree**.
